name: transcript-video
description: Generate synced transcript videos from audio files with audio-reactive visualizations, concept tagging, and instrument panel dataviz

readme.html:
transcript-video

Generate synced transcript videos from audio files. Word-by-word text synchronized to audio with semantic phrase splitting, concept highlighting, and audio-reactive instrument panel visualizations.

https://github.com/ejfox/transcript-video

What it does

Give it an audio file. Get back a 1080p video with:

  • Synced transcript — words appear timed to speech, split on natural phrase boundaries
  • Concept tagging — highlight key terms in color (everything else is monochrome)
  • Audio visualizations — spectrogram, waveforms, oscilloscopes at multiple time scales, Lissajous phase scope, EKG history strips, waterfall display
  • QR codes — scannable provenance metadata per phrase (speaker, timestamp, context)
  • Instrument panel layout — labeled modules with metadata (timebase, gain, frequency range, live dBFS readout)

Designed for video essay production — turn podcast clips, interviews, and lectures into footage you can drop into your NLE.

Install

npm install -g transcript-video

# Or clone and link
git clone https://github.com/ejfox/transcript-video.git
cd transcript-video
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
npm link

Requirements:

  • Node.js 18+
  • ffmpeg
  • OPENAI_API_KEY env var (for Whisper word-level timestamps, ~$0.006/min)

Usage

# Basic — transcribe + render
transcript-video interview.mp3

# With a speaker label
transcript-video podcast.mp3 --speaker "ERIK DAVIS — HARVARD CSWR"

# First 5 minutes only
transcript-video lecture.mp3 --duration 300

# 4K output
transcript-video clip.mp3 --width 3840 --height 2160

# Preview in browser (no render)
transcript-video clip.mp3 --preview

# Use pre-existing word timestamps (skip API call)
transcript-video clip.mp3 --words clip-words.json

# Custom concept tags
transcript-video clip.mp3 --tags my-tags.json

Concept tags

Create a JSON file with word clusters to highlight. Only tagged words get color — everything else stays monochrome.

[
  {
    "label": "consciousness",
    "color": "#6eedf7",
    "words": ["consciousness", "sentient", "aware", "mind", "qualia"]
  },
  {
    "label": "technical",
    "color": "#ffaa00",
    "words": ["model", "training", "alignment", "neural", "parameter"]
  }
]

Pass it with --tags my-tags.json. Word matching is case-insensitive and supports partial stems (e.g., "meditat" matches "meditation", "meditative", "meditating").

How it works

  1. Transcription — Sends audio to OpenAI Whisper API with timestamp_granularities[]=word to get per-word timing
  2. Phrase splitting — Groups words into natural phrases based on pauses, punctuation, and clause boundaries (not mechanical N-word chunks)
  3. Frame rendering — Playwright opens the template in headless Chrome, steps through time frame-by-frame, injects pre-decoded PCM audio data into the visualization engine, and screenshots each frame
  4. Encoding — ffmpeg assembles frames + original audio into h264/aac MP4

Render speed is ~20-25fps (a 10-minute clip takes ~12 minutes to render).

Options

FlagDefaultDescription
--words <file>autoPre-computed word timestamps JSON
--tags <file>built-inConcept tag definitions
--speaker "LABEL"filenameSpeaker label on screen
--duration <sec>fullRender only first N seconds
--width <px>1920Output width
--height <px>1080Output height
--fps <num>30Frames per second
--output <file><input>-transcript.mp4Output path
--previewfalseOpen in browser instead of rendering

Design

  • Monochrome — all visualizations are white-on-black. Color is reserved exclusively for concept-tagged words
  • Instrument panel — each visualization module is a labeled panel with metadata annotations (timebase, gain, frequency range)
  • Fonts — Archivo Black (display), Monaspace Xenon (prose), Monaspace Krypton (labels) via CDN
  • 16:9 locked — always renders at correct aspect ratio for YouTube/FCP

License

MIT
readme.raw:
# transcript-video

Generate synced transcript videos from audio files. Word-by-word text synchronized to audio with semantic phrase splitting, concept highlighting, and audio-reactive instrument panel visualizations.

https://github.com/ejfox/transcript-video

![transcript-video demo](https://img.shields.io/badge/1080p-h264%2Baac-blue) ![MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green)

## What it does

Give it an audio file. Get back a 1080p video with:

- **Synced transcript** — words appear timed to speech, split on natural phrase boundaries
- **Concept tagging** — highlight key terms in color (everything else is monochrome)
- **Audio visualizations** — spectrogram, waveforms, oscilloscopes at multiple time scales, Lissajous phase scope, EKG history strips, waterfall display
- **QR codes** — scannable provenance metadata per phrase (speaker, timestamp, context)
- **Instrument panel layout** — labeled modules with metadata (timebase, gain, frequency range, live dBFS readout)

Designed for video essay production — turn podcast clips, interviews, and lectures into footage you can drop into your NLE.

## Install

```bash
npm install -g transcript-video

# Or clone and link
git clone https://github.com/ejfox/transcript-video.git
cd transcript-video
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
npm link
```

**Requirements:**
- Node.js 18+
- ffmpeg
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var (for Whisper word-level timestamps, ~$0.006/min)

## Usage

```bash
# Basic — transcribe + render
transcript-video interview.mp3

# With a speaker label
transcript-video podcast.mp3 --speaker "ERIK DAVIS — HARVARD CSWR"

# First 5 minutes only
transcript-video lecture.mp3 --duration 300

# 4K output
transcript-video clip.mp3 --width 3840 --height 2160

# Preview in browser (no render)
transcript-video clip.mp3 --preview

# Use pre-existing word timestamps (skip API call)
transcript-video clip.mp3 --words clip-words.json

# Custom concept tags
transcript-video clip.mp3 --tags my-tags.json
```

## Concept tags

Create a JSON file with word clusters to highlight. Only tagged words get color — everything else stays monochrome.

```json
[
  {
    "label": "consciousness",
    "color": "#6eedf7",
    "words": ["consciousness", "sentient", "aware", "mind", "qualia"]
  },
  {
    "label": "technical",
    "color": "#ffaa00",
    "words": ["model", "training", "alignment", "neural", "parameter"]
  }
]
```

Pass it with `--tags my-tags.json`. Word matching is case-insensitive and supports partial stems (e.g., `"meditat"` matches "meditation", "meditative", "meditating").

## How it works

1. **Transcription** — Sends audio to OpenAI Whisper API with `timestamp_granularities[]=word` to get per-word timing
2. **Phrase splitting** — Groups words into natural phrases based on pauses, punctuation, and clause boundaries (not mechanical N-word chunks)
3. **Frame rendering** — Playwright opens the template in headless Chrome, steps through time frame-by-frame, injects pre-decoded PCM audio data into the visualization engine, and screenshots each frame
4. **Encoding** — ffmpeg assembles frames + original audio into h264/aac MP4

Render speed is ~20-25fps (a 10-minute clip takes ~12 minutes to render).

## Options

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--words <file>` | auto | Pre-computed word timestamps JSON |
| `--tags <file>` | built-in | Concept tag definitions |
| `--speaker "LABEL"` | filename | Speaker label on screen |
| `--duration <sec>` | full | Render only first N seconds |
| `--width <px>` | 1920 | Output width |
| `--height <px>` | 1080 | Output height |
| `--fps <num>` | 30 | Frames per second |
| `--output <file>` | `<input>-transcript.mp4` | Output path |
| `--preview` | false | Open in browser instead of rendering |

## Design

- **Monochrome** — all visualizations are white-on-black. Color is reserved exclusively for concept-tagged words
- **Instrument panel** — each visualization module is a labeled panel with metadata annotations (timebase, gain, frequency range)
- **Fonts** — Archivo Black (display), Monaspace Xenon (prose), Monaspace Krypton (labels) via CDN
- **16:9 locked** — always renders at correct aspect ratio for YouTube/FCP

## License

MIT
